tools.name: get_image
tools.description: Get metadata (CDN URL, size, MIME type, dimensions) for one PixelVault image by id. Maps to GET /v1/images/:id. Requires a PixelVault API key sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. The returned CDN URL supports on-the-fly transforms via query params — e.g. ?w=400&fit=cover (resize/crop)

tools.name: list_images
tools.description: List images in your PixelVault project, most recent first. Maps to GET /v1/images with pagination. Requires a PixelVault API key sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. The returned CDN URL supports on-the-fly transforms via query params — e.g. ?w=400&fit=cover (resize/crop), ?fmt=webp (

tools.name: delete_image
tools.description: Permanently delete one PixelVault image by id. Maps to DELETE /v1/images/:id. Requires a PixelVault API key sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

tools.name: upload_image
tools.description: Upload an image to PixelVault and get an instant CDN URL. Maps to POST /v1/images. Provide exactly one of `source_url` (a public http(s) URL the server fetches) or `data` (base64-encoded bytes); optional `folder`, `filename`, and `expires_in` (seconds, for an auto-expiring image). Max 5 MB; JPG/PNG/

tools.name: upload_batch
tools.description: Upload many images (1–50) in one call, grouped into a collection — e.g. a CI run or a set of generated variants. Maps to POST /v1/images/batch. Each item is `data` (base64) or `source_url` (server-fetched, SSRF-guarded), with optional `filename`/`metadata`. Set `visibility: "private"` to get a signe

tools.name: sign_url
tools.description: Mint a time-limited signed URL for a private image. Maps to POST /v1/images/:id/sign-url. Provide `id` and optional `expires_in` (seconds, default 3600). The signature binds the image, so the URL can't be replayed against another image; strip it and the CDN returns 403. Deleting the image revokes it

tools.name: transform_image
tools.description: Build an on-the-fly transform URL for a PixelVault image (resize, crop, format/quality, AI background removal, blur/sharpen/rotate/flip, brightness/contrast/saturation, and same-project watermark tiling). Provide exactly one of `url` (a PixelVault CDN URL) or `id` (an image id, resolved via the API

tools.name: rescue_imgur
tools.description: Scan a web page for hotlinked Imgur images and return a PixelVault rescue URL for each — a proxy that lazily rehosts the image on first request, keeping it working through the UK Imgur block. The anonymous rescue tier is a durable ~30-day edge cache (see `cache_ttl_days` in the result), not permanen

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_image" references sensitive artifacts (authorization, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_image, list_images, upload_image, upload_batch, rescue_imgur · Private data: get_image, list_images, delete_image, upload_image, upload_batch, sign_url, transform_image, rescue_imgur · Public sink: upload_image, upload_batch, sign_url, rescue_imgur · Destructive: get_image, list_images, delete_image, upload_image, upload_batch, transform_image, rescue_imgur.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_image, list_images, upload_image, upload_batch, rescue_imgur · Private data: get_image, list_images, delete_image, upload_image, upload_batch, sign_url, transform_image, rescue_imgur · Public sink: upload_image, upload_batch, sign_url, rescue_imgur · Destructive: get_image, list_images, delete_image, upload_image, upload_batch, transform_image, rescue_imgur

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
